colors.notes: PB29 + Quin Gold blend per DS. Granulating, semi-transparent. Verified: danielsmith.com / Wet Paint.
colors.ace_note: Kelpy, moody, and a little theatrical — ultramarine sinks while gold floats. Gorgeous for shadows that feel alive, not muddy. Pairs dangerously well with Moonglow.
colors.best_for: Underwater greens, kelp forests, cool shadow foliage, and teal-leaning atmospheric passages.
colors.ace_history: Undersea Green pairs ultramarine with quinacridone gold — a contemporary DS blend aimed at marine and botanical depth rather than a single historical pigment name.

mix_tips.result: Bioluminescent dusk water — violet and teal granulate in layers.
Abyssal blue-green; both granulate for textured deep water.
Cool moonlit surf with floating blue halos.

colors.toxicity_habit: Nickel-containing pigment — wash hands after sessions; no eating at the desk.

colors.notes: PBr7. Daniel Smith: Semi-Transparent, Granulating Yes, Non-staining. Lightfastness Excellent.
colors.ace_note: Your warm-weather sketching workhorse. DS version granulates nicely; lean into it for tree bark and sun-warmed skin tones.
colors.best_for: Tree bark, warm skin undertones, earthy shadows, and granulating mid-tones in plein-air sketches.
colors.ace_history: Burnt sienna is calcined natural iron oxide — a portrait and landscape staple since the 18th century, when earth pigments anchored the warm side of every palette.

mix_tips.result: Granulating greys and soft storm skies — the textbook earth + ultramarine neutral.
Deep olive-greens for foliage shadows without going muddy black.
Sunlit leaves and mossy mid-tones — warm earth anchors bright greens.

colors.notes: 3-pigment blend: Anthraquinoid Red + Ultramarine + Viridian. Transparent, granulating. Verified: Wet Paint / DS.
colors.ace_note: The drama queen. Three pigments having a party on wet paper — violet, blue-green halos, granulation everywhere. Skies, snow shadows, anything that needs magic hour.
colors.best_for: Night skies, snow shadows, magical granulating washes, and moody backgrounds where one pigment does the choreography.
colors.ace_history: Moonglow is a modern Daniel Smith hero — a deliberate three-pigment dance (anthraquinoid red, ultramarine, viridian) built for granulation on the paper, not for copying a single historic tube.

mix_tips.result: Warm-violet storm clouds with earthy granulation — classic moody sky recipe.
Deep blue-violet galaxies; ultramarine pushes the shadow side cooler.
Kelpy twilight water — green and violet separate beautifully wet-on-wet.

colors.notes: Coelinblau. Semi-opaque. Horadam catalog. Color-check Jul 8 2026 — half-pan photo.
colors.ace_note: Milky, soft, a little stubborn — won't mix into screaming brights, but skies love it. Semi-opaque means it can rescue washed-out washes.
colors.ace_history: Cerulean blue (PB35) was synthesized in the 1860s as a safer stand-in for cobalt sky blues — Turner and the Impressionists chased this milky, non-staining hue for distant horizons.

colors.notes: Heliotürkis. Semi-transparent. Verified: Jackson's Art / Schmincke PB16. Color-check Jul 8 2026 — half-pan photo.
colors.ace_note: Clean tropical water without the phthalo aggression. Bright but behaves — tropical seas, ceramic glazes, fresh spring foliage highlights.
colors.ace_history: Helio turquoise is Schmincke's phthalocyanine turquoise (PB16) — a 20th-century pigment that gave poster designers and watercolorists a clean tropical blue-green without grinding gemstones.

colors.notes: Phthalogrün. Transparent, high staining. Color-check Jul 8 2026 — half-pan photo.
colors.ace_note: A tiny squeeze goes a mile. Staining, transparent, powerful — respect the ratio or it will eat your whole painting.
colors.best_for: Mixing clean greens, deep foliage shadows, and turquoise-leaning accents — a staining powerhouse.
colors.ace_history: Phthalocyanine green (PG7) arrived in the 20th century and revolutionized mixing — one tiny touch shifts any yellow into vivid natural green.

mix_tips.result: Bright spring meadow greens — clean and legible.
Deep forest shadow greens without black.
Chartreuse and acid spring leaves — use sparingly.

colors.notes: Maigrün. Mixed phthalo + yellow. Color-check Jul 8 2026 — half-pan photo.

colors.best_for: Spring leaves, mixed greens straight from the tube, and cheerful landscape passages.
colors.ace_history: May green is a classic convenience mixture (often yellow + phthalo) named for spring foliage — a staple on European plein-air palettes.

mix_tips.result: Natural tree-canopy greens with warm trunks nearby.
Pastoral olive-greens for hills and hedgerows.
Soft greyed greens for distant tree lines.

colors.notes: Kobaltgrün tief. Color-check Jul 8 2026 — half-pan photo.

colors.notes: Zitronengelb. Transparent. Color-check Jul 8 2026 — half-pan photo.

caveats: Swatch colors are approximations — real paint shifts with water, paper, and layering.
Chinese names on Schmincke are thoughtful translations; Daniel Smith & MaimeriBlu come from your tube labels.
Silver and Brilliant Opera Rose are specialty colors — mixing may surprise you.
Mix Lab is brainstorm mode, not lab accuracy. Always do a test swatch before committing.
